text.tspan: MONOLITHIC GraphQL
(one GraphQL server that wraps all backend services)

g.text: GRAPHQL FEDERATION (Apollo Federation v2, Cosmo, Hive)

text.tspan: WHEN TO USE WHICH:
u2022 <10 services, one team u2192 MONOLITHIC GraphQL
u2022 <30 services, few teams u2192 GraphQL PER BFF
u2022 50+ services, many teams u2192 FEDERATION
u2022 high integration with non-GraphQL world u2192 plain BFFs + API composition
One central team owns the schema and resolvers.
Every field addition goes through that team u2192 bottleneck.
Good for small orgs; collapses at scale.
Gateway introspects all subgraph schemas, merges them.
Where types overlap, gateway needs CONFIG to resolve conflicts.
Works but coupling is heavy; deprecated in favor of Federation v1+v2.
Subgraphs declare their pieces via DIRECTIVES (@key, @external, @extends).
Gateway composes them deterministically into a supergraph.
Each team owns its slice; cross-cutting reference via shared entity keys.
u2192 the current dominant approach
Each client (web, mobile) has its OWN GraphQL BFF.
Web-BFF and Mobile-BFF have different schemas (tailored).
Both might call the same backend microservices internally.
u2192 popular alternative when federation is overkill
